message.reasoning:
We need to produce JSON response with kind think|tool_call|complete. We are in prepare_to_edit stage: need to gather evidence before editing schema definitions. We need to expand file symbols for crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs. The graph frontier suggests expand_file [F1] with priority=112 candidates=17. So we should call session.expand_file with target selector for that file.

We must select short id for the node. The file is likely represented as a node with some short id. In the graph frontier they refer to [F1] as relation? Actually [F1] is a node? Probably the file node has short id like F1? In the graph relations they list [F1] as crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs --exports--> [S1] etc. So the file node may be identified as something like F1. The frontier says expand_file [F1] relation=- direction=- candidates=17 priority=112 via session.expand_file(...). So we should use session.expand_file with target selector equal to that short id, likely "F1". Use payload: {"target_selector":"F1"} maybe plus optional fields.

We need to return JSON with kind tool_call, tool_call tool_name "session.expand_file", payload. Also acquired_fact_ids list of fact ids we acquire? We may not have any yet. Could leave empty array. readiness_state probably "evidence_acquired"? Actually we are about to call tool to expand file, so we are not ready yet. Could set readiness_state "evidence_acquired"? But we haven't acquired yet. Maybe "evidence_visible"? Actually we are about to request expansion, so we are not ready to edit yet. Use "not_ready". The schema: readiness_state can be "not_ready|evidence_visible|evidence_acquired|ready_to_edit". Since we are about to call tool, we are not ready yet. So set readiness_state "not_ready". Provide assistant_message brief status.

Thus JSON:

{
  "kind":"tool_call",
  "assistant_message":"Expanding file symbols for artifacts.rs to gather evidence",
  "tool_call":{"tool_name":"session.expand_file","payload":{"target_selector":"F1"}},
  "acquired_fact_ids":[],
  "readiness_state":"not_ready"
}

Make sure no extra fields. Also ensure proper JSON formatting, no trailing commas. Provide exactly that.
